subject: Political Science
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the meaning and importance of democratic outcomes
Explain how democracy produces accountable and responsive government
Understand the relationship between democracy and economic development
Analyse issues of inequality and poverty in democratic societies
Understand how democracy accommodates social diversity
Recognize the role of democracy in promoting dignity and freedom
Develop critical thinking about strengths and limitations of democracy

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Democratic Outcomes
topics_covered: Democracy as a form of government
student_activities: List expectations people have from democracy

lesson_plan.title: Accountable and Responsive Government
student_activities: Identify ways citizens can question government decisions

lesson_plan.title: Legitimacy of Democratic Government
teaching_strategy: Use of survey examples from the chapter
student_activities: Write reasons why elections are important

lesson_plan.title: Reduction of Inequality and Poverty
student_activities: Identify examples of inequality in society

lesson_plan.title: Accommodation of Social Diversity
topics_covered: Role of democracy in resolving conflicts
Importance of majority and minority balance
student_activities: Discuss examples of social diversity in society

lesson_plan.title: Dignity, Freedom and Chapter Revision
student_activities: Discuss examples of freedom and equality

teacher_tip: Encourage students to relate democratic concepts to real-life examples such as elections, rights, and government policies so they can better understand how democracy works in everyday life.
